conversation.text: Process incoming customer reviews from our public form.
A few things to settle before I build. How will reviews arrive — webhook on submission, polled from somewhere, or something else? And what should the workflow do with each review?
Webhook on each submission. The form posts to /new-review with fields: customer_name, email, rating (1-5), text.
Should I treat all reviews the same, or branch based on the rating?
Branch. For 4-5 stars, post a celebration message to Slack #wins. For 1-2 stars, create a Linear issue in our 'support' team AND send an apology email to the customer at the email they submitted.
Drop them. No action needed for middling reviews.
Plan: Webhook → Switch on rating. ≥4 → Slack #wins. ≤2 → Linear in support team + email customer. =3 → no-op. Approve?

executionScenarios.name: positive-review
executionScenarios.description: A 5-star review fires the Slack celebration branch only
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives a POST at /new-review with body { customer_name: 'Carlos', email: 'carlos@example.com', rating: 5, text: 'Best product I have used this year.' }. The Slack node returns a success response. The Linear and email nodes, if reached, would also return successes.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow runs without errors. A Slack post is sent to the #wins channel containing some mention of the customer name or review. NO Linear issue is created. NO email is sent. The Switch node routed correctly based on rating=5.

executionScenarios.name: negative-review
executionScenarios.description: A 2-star review fires both the Linear and email branches, but NOT the Slack #wins branch
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives a POST at /new-review with body { customer_name: 'Dana', email: 'dana@example.com', rating: 2, text: 'Setup was confusing and support was slow.' }. The Linear and email nodes return success responses.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow runs without errors. A Linear issue is created in the 'support' team. An email is sent to 'dana@example.com'. NO Slack post is sent to #wins — the Switch routed away from that branch because rating=2.

executionScenarios.name: middling-review
executionScenarios.description: A 3-star review hits the no-op branch; no notifications fire
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives a POST at /new-review with body { customer_name: 'Emma', email: 'emma@example.com', rating: 3, text: 'It is fine. Could be better.' }. All service nodes, if reached, would return successes.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow runs without errors. NO Slack post is sent. NO Linear issue is created. NO email is sent. The Switch node correctly routed the 3-star review to a dead-end / no-op branch.
